Which of the following functions can be performed by the Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) Control which is available under Control Panel {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Administrative Tools {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Computer Management in Windows7?

  1. You can configure the WMI Control to back up your WMI repository.
  2. You can change the default namespace that is targeted in WMI scripts.
  3. You can change hardware configuration settings.
  4. You can roll back to the previous version of a driver.
questions.explanation: You can configure the WMI Control to back up your WMI repository.

How do you add programs from the Start menu to Taskbar's Quick Launch area in Windows 7?

  1. Just drag and drop the program you want to add to the Quick Launch area on the Taskbar from the Start menu.
  2. Right-click the program you want to add to the Quick Launch area on the Taskbar from the Start menu and select Pin to Taskbar.

  4. There is no method to add a program from the Start menu to the Quick Launch area on the Taskbar.

Which of the following is not a valid feature of Disk Management which is available under Control Panel {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Administrative Tools {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Computer Management in Windows 7?

  1. It allows you to create a basic, spanned or striped partition directly from the menu.
  2. It allows you to extend and shrink partitions directly from the windows interface.
  3. It allows you disk conversion options whereby you can convert the disk to dynamic partition style or to GUID partition table when you add more than four partitions to a basic disk.
  4. It allows you to schedule a task to run in response to an event.
questions.explanation: It allows you to schedule a task to run in response to an event.

questions.question:
What does the Snipping Tool do in Windows 7?

  1. It is used to capture screenshots of any object on your screen.
  2. It is used to perform all kinds of quick calculations.
  3. It is used to create and edit the graphic files you work with.
  4. It is used to record sounds via your computer’s built-in microphone.
questions.explanation: It is used to capture screenshots of any object on your screen.

questions.question:
How do you adjust the Taskbar location on the Windows 7 screen?

  1. You can’t adjust the Taskbar location on the Windows 7 screen.
  2. Right-click on any empty area of the Taskbar and select the Properties option from the list. In the Taskbar tab, click on the Taskbar location on screen and set the location you want.
  3. Go to the Control Panel from the Start menu and click on the Taskbar and Start Menu icon and set the Taskbar location.
  4. left-click on any empty area of the Taskbar and select the Properties option from the list. In the Taskbar tab, click on the Taskbar location on screen and set the location you want.
questions.explanation: Right-click on any empty area of the Taskbar and select the Properties option from the list. In the Taskbar tab, click on the Taskbar location on screen and set the location you want.

questions.question:
What is the function of the Aero Peek feature in Windows 7?

  1. It is used to capture screenshots of anything on your desktop, Like a picture or a section of a webpage.
  2. It allows you to see your desktop without minimising all your currently opened windows.
  3. It is used to create and edit the graphics files you work with.
  4. It is used to perform all kinds of quick calculations.
questions.explanation: It allows you to see your desktop without minimising all your currently opened windows.

questions.question:
What result will the search criteria tropical and island yield in Windows 7 library?

  1. It will find files that contain either of the words tropical or island.
  2. It will find files that contain neither the word tropical nor the word island.
  3. It will find files that contain both of the words tropical and island.
  4. It will find files that contain only the word tropical.
questions.explanation: It will find files that contain either of the words tropical or island.

A computer is free from tiredness, monotony, etc., reflects which characteristic?
questions.explanation: Unlike human beings, a computer is free from monotony, tiredness, lack of concentration etc, and can work for hours without creating any errors. This characteristic of computer is known as diligence.

questions.question: It is responsible for all numerical and logical calculations.
questions.explanation: ALU is a digital circuit in which numerical and logical operations are defined.

questions.question: Out of the following, whom do you think is the most probable user of a light pen?
questions.explanation: Light pen is useful for graphic designer as it is useful for identifying a particular location on the screen.

questions.question: Input device is used to make a digital copy of a photograph.
questions.explanation: Scanner is an input device that is used to make a digital copy of a photograph. It converts the image or text into a digital file.

questions.question: Which of the following does not represent an I/O device?
questions.explanation: CU is used to coordinate the input and the output devices of a computer system.

questions.question: ________ is a medium size scanner which has rolling drum to scan your documents.
Large amounts of cheques are processed by using
questions.explanation: Large amount of cheques are processed by the MICR (Magnetic Ink Character Recognition). It can read characters that are printed using a special magnetic ink. It is mostly used in banks.

questions.question: What does OCR stand for?
questions.explanation: OCR stands for Optical Character Precognition used as an input device in a computer system.

questions.question: Input device used in evaluating answer sheets by a teacher.
questions.explanation: OMR (Optical Mark Recognition) is the process of capturing human-marked data from document forms such as surveys and test.

questions.question: Which type of monitor works in the same way as a television?
questions.explanation: CRT monitor works in the same way as a television.

questions.question: Dot matrix is a type of
questions.explanation: Dot matrix is a type of impact printer that prints one character at a time.

questions.question: Output device is used to generate the long map of building and shopping malls.
questions.explanation: Plotter is a computer hardware device similar to a printer that uses a pen, pencil, marker or other writing tools to make a design.

questions.question: ________ memory is used to store large amount of information permanently.
questions.explanation: Secondary memory is used to store large amount of information permanently.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of non-volatile memory?
questions.explanation: Among given options, ROM is the memory that retains its contents even after the interruption of power so, it is a non-volatile memory.

questions.question: Which of the following storage device can be used for storing large backup data?
questions.explanation: Magnetic tape is used with larger computers such as the mainframe, where huge amount of data is kept stored for longer period of time.

questions.question: Pen drive is also known as
questions.explanation: A USB flash drive, data stick, thumb drive or pen drive is a portable drive that connects to a computer’s USB port.

questions.question: The largest unit of memory is
questions.explanation: Geopbyte is the highest memory measurement unit. It is equal to 1024 Brontobyte, (nearly 2100 bytes).

questions.question: Data by itself is not useful unless
questions.explanation: Data is unprocessed and raw facts. It is not useful unless it is processed to obtain information.

questions.question: For taking decisions, data must be
questions.explanation: For taking decisions, data must be processed and manipulated correctly.

questions.question: The data which is accurate, specific and organised for a purpose is known as
questions.explanation: Data after processing becomes information and it is accurate, specific and organised for a purpose.

questions.question: Information is
questions.explanation: Information is the processed form of data.

questions.question: An operating system manages
questions.explanation: An operating system manages disk and I/O devices as it is responsible for input from keyboard, mouse, etc. It also manages the computer memory, so that each process can run most effectively.

questions.question: Along with knowledge, if someone has common sense to know when and how to use that knowledge then it is known as
questions.explanation: Wisdom is applied knowledge and it has a capacity to choose when and how to use knowledge.

questions.question: When the content offers interactivity and users can control the progress of the content, it is called as
questions.explanation: Non-linear multimedia uses interactivity to control progress, e.g. video games.

questions.question: An operating system of a computer serves as a software interface between the user and the
questions.explanation: An operating system of a computer serves as a software interface between the user and the hardware.

questions.question: Interactive combination of text, graphics, images, audio, video etc., is known as
questions.explanation: Multimedia is a interactive combination of text, animations, sounds, images etc.

questions.question: Suppose you have bought all hardware components required for assembling a computer. After assembling the computer system, which will be the very first software to be installed?
questions.explanation: Operating system will be the very first software to be installed after assembling the computer system.

questions.question: Which of the following is a component of multimedia?
questions.explanation: Text, images, audio all are the components of multimedia.

questions.question: The content like audio, video and animation along with text is called
questions.explanation: It is an extension of hypertext. Hypermedia can include text, audio, images, sounds etc.

questions.question: Find out the odd one
questions.explanation: WAVE is an audio format, rest are image file extensions.

questions.question: Which of the following is like a heart of operating system?
questions.explanation: Kernel is like a heart o f operating system which plays a vital role in modern OS.

questions.question: What should be the minimum capacity of the RAM for multimedia?
questions.explanation: Atleast 64 MB RAM is needed for multimedia.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an OS?
questions.explanation: Apple is not an OS while it is the name of a company.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are mobile operating system?
questions.explanation: Both Android and Symbian are two different mobile operating systems.

questions.question: Oreo 8.0.0 is the version of
________ reduces the Kernel code size and increases the security.
questions.explanation: Micro Kernel reduces the Kernel code size and also increases the security and stability of an operating system.

questions.question: Which of the following communicates with the device through the computer bus?
questions.explanation: A device driver is a small piece of software which communicates with the device through the computer bus.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are types of special files?
questions.explanation: Special files are of two types as Character special file and Block special file.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a set of instructions that tells the hardware how to perform the tasks?
questions.explanation: Software is a set of instructions that tells the hardware how to perform the tasks.

questions.question: Operating system is a
questions.explanation: Operating system is a system software that acts as an interface between the user and the hardware.

questions.question: Which software takes control of computer system on startup?
questions.explanation: Operating system takes control of computer system on startup.

questions.question: Which device is used to convert the program written in assembly language into machine language?
questions.explanation: Assembler is used to convert the program written in assembly language into machine language because it is a language processor that converts the user defined language into a form which can be easily understood by the computer.

questions.question: Interpreter converts a high level language program into a machine language by
questions.explanation: Interpreter is a language processor, which converts the high level language program into machine language by using line-by-line mechanism.

questions.question: The language processor which converts high level language into machine language is
questions.explanation: Both compiler and interpreter are language processors that converts high level language into machine language.

questions.question: Computer connected with LAN
questions.explanation: Computer connected with LAN can share information or peripheral devices.

questions.question: Utility programs include:
questions.explanation: Utility programs includes virus scanning, backup, disk defragmenter software.

questions.question: Which type of software is designed to perform specific, personal, business or scientific processing tasks?
questions.explanation: Application software is a computer software which is designed to help the user to perform some specific or personal tasks.

questions.question: Which of the following is not related to a utility software?
questions.explanation: Railway reservation system is an example of application software whereas, rest all are software utilities.

questions.question: A group of computers connected together with the help of cables within an office building is called
questions.explanation: Using LAN, we can connect computers within a building only.

questions.question: Which of the following is not related to an application software?
questions.explanation: Application software is a software which carries out a specific task like Word processor, spreadsheet, DBMS etc. Among the given options, operating system is related to system software.

questions.question: DTP is a tool for graphic designers and non-designers to create visual communications for professional. DTP stands for
Network formed between computers which are spread across the continents is called
questions.explanation: Across the continents, computers can be connected using WAN only.

questions.question: Which of the following uses cables to connect two or more computers?
questions.explanation: In guided media, signals are guided through cables.

questions.question: Assembly language is a low level programming language

options.option: that is not limited by the computer or for one specific job
sometimes referred to as machine code or object code
makes use of mnemonics to interact with computer system

questions.explanation: Assembly language is a low level programming language that uses mnemonics to interact with computer system.

questions.question: Machine language sometimes referred to as

options.option: language that is not limited by the computer.
programming language that is used widely by the programmer.
programming language that is more easily understood.

questions.explanation: Machine language sometimes referred to as machine code or object code because it is the language which is only understood by the computer as a computer itself is just a machine or an electronic device.

questions.question: Optical fibres are made up of thin strands of
questions.explanation: Optical fibres are made up of thin strands of glass like material.

questions.question: Which type of connector does a Ethernet cable use?
questions.explanation: RJ-45 is most commonly used to connect computers onto Ethernet based local area network.

questions.question: For using Wi-Fi, which of the following is not required?
questions.explanation: Bluetooth is not required for Wi-Fi connection.

questions.question: Modulation and demodulation is performed by
questions.explanation: Modulation and demodulation is performed by modem.

questions.question: A modem is connected in between a telephone line and a
questions.explanation: Modem is a device that enables computers and other equipments to communicate with each other using telephone lines.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a transmission medium?
questions.explanation: All are communication channel but modem is a device that converts analog signals to digital signals or vice-versa.

questions.question: What is the unit used to measure the data which is transmitted through modem?
questions.explanation: Bits per second is the unit used to measure the data through modem.

questions.question: Which of the following protocol is also known as Remote Login?
questions.explanation: Telnet is also known as Remote Login, used for creating a connection with a remote computer.

questions.question: What can you do with the Internet?

options.option: Find diverse perspective on issues from a global audience
Access pictures, sounds, video clips and other media elements
Internet exchange information, access pictures, find diverse perspective on issue from a global audience.

questions.explanation: Internet exchange information, access pictures, find diverse perspective on issues from a global audience.

questions.question: A set of rules that governs data communication is
questions.explanation: A protocol is a set of rules that governs the communication between computers on a network.

questions.question: Which part of TCP/IP is responsible for dividing a file or message into very small parts, at the source computer?
questions.explanation: TCP part breaks the messages into smaller packets which are transmitted over the Internet.

questions.question: FTP is referred to as
questions.explanation: FTP is a protocol through which Internet users can upload files from their computer to a Website. In which, FTP stands for File Transfer Protocol.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not an example of raster image?
questions.explanation: CGM is not an example of raster image. Raster formats store images as bitmaps (also known as pixmaps). These image formats (RIFs) should be the most familiar to Internet users.

questions.question: SVG stands for:
questions.explanation: Scalable Vector Graphics is an XML-based vector image format for two-dimensional graphics with support for interactivity and animation. The SVG specification is an open standard developed by the World Wide Web Consortium since 1999.

questions.question: Vector images are defined by:
questions.explanation: Vector graphics, as a form of computer graphics, is the set of mechanisms for creating visual images directly from geometric shapes defined on a Cartesian plane, such as points, lines, curves, and polygons.

questions.question: 8 bits per pixel will give you:
questions.explanation: 8-bit color graphics are a method of storing image information in a computer's memory or in an image file, so that each pixel is represented by 8-bits. The maximum number of colors that can be displayed at any one time is 256 or 28.

questions.question: 3D animation is used in:
questions.explanation: Computer animation is the process used for digitally generating animated images.

questions.question: 2D animation is used in:
questions.explanation: Cartoons and movies that we see normally on television or big screens are 2D animation.

questions.question: Real Video is developed by:
questions.explanation: RealVideo is a suite of proprietary video compression formats developed by RealNetworks - the specific format changes with the version. It was first released in 1997 and as of 2008 was at version 10. RealVideo is supported on many platforms, including Windows, Mac, Linux, Solaris, and several mobile phones.

questions.question: ________ used for video conferencing.
DPI means:
questions.explanation: Dots per inch is a measure of spatial printing, video or image scanner dot density, in particular the number of individual dots that can be placed in a line within the span of 1 inch.

questions.question: AU and AIFF are the examples of:
questions.explanation: An audio file format is a container format for storing audio data on a computer system. The general approach towards storing digital audio is to sample the audio voltage which, on playback, would correspond to a certain position of the membrane in a speaker of the individual channels with a certain resolution - the number of bits per sample - in regular intervals (forming the sample rate). This data can then be stored uncompressed, or compressed to reduce the file size.

questions.question: CGM and SVG are the examples of:
questions.explanation: The CGM format also has reliable data exchange capabilities, resulting in excellent interoperability. The SVG format does not have the data exchange credentials of CGM. SVG was primarily designed to view vector graphics in a web browser.

questions.question: CGM stands for:
questions.explanation: Computer Graphics Metafile (CGM) is a free and open international standard file format for 2D vector graphics, raster graphics, and text, and is defined by ISO/IEC 8632.

questions.question: ________ is the internal storage area, which holds the data and instructions during the processing, and keeps it saved for future use.
questions.explanation: Memory is the internal storage area which holds the data and instructions.

questions.question: Computer memory contains microchips of:
questions.explanation: Computer memory contains microchips of Random Access Memory(RAM), Read Only Memory(ROM), Programmable Read Only Memory (PROM).

questions.question: Which of the following is used as a primary storage device?
questions.explanation: Programmable Read Only Memory (PROM) allows a chip to be programmed by the user for converting critical and lengthy operations into microprograms that are fused into a chip. It is a type of ROM.

questions.question: EEPROM stands for:
questions.explanation: Electrically Erasable and Programmable Read Only Memory (EEPROM) can be reprogrammed with special electrical pulses.

questions.question: RAM is used as small memory since it is:
questions.explanation: RAM is a volatile memory as data is not stored permanently in it. Data is erased as power is turned off.

questions.question: Which of the following is a unit of measurement used with computer system?
questions.explanation: There are different units of measurement in a computer like nibble, bytes, megabytes, gigabytes, terabytes, zettabytes, etc.

questions.question: The part of the computer, which can be touched is called:
questions.explanation:
Hardware is that part of the computer system that we can touch and feel.
For example: keyboard, monitor, mouse, scanner, and many more.

questions.question: What is a set of instructions that directs the computer to process information?
questions.explanation: Computer hardware is only as effective as the instructions we give it, and those instructions are contained in software. Software not only directs the computer to manage its internal resources but also enables the user to tailor a computer system to provide specific business value.

questions.question: Compiler is a:
questions.explanation: The translation of a high-level language program to object code is accomplished by a software program called a compiler, which translates the entire program at once.

questions.question: What translates each source program statement into a sequence of machine instructions and then executes these machine instructions before translating the next resource language statement?
questions.explanation: An interpreter translates and executes one source program statement at a time. Because this translation is done one statement at a time, interpreters tend to be simpler than compilers. This simplicity allows for more extensive debugging and diagnostic aids to be available on interpreters.

questions.question: Disk Defragmentation Software is a(an):
questions.explanation: Disk defragmenters can detect computer files whose contents are stored on the hard disk in disjointed fragments, and move the fragments together to increase efficiency.

questions.question: Trace mechanism is a:
questions.explanation: Trace mechanism is a debugging tool as it is used to remove errors.

questions.question: Disk Cleanup helps to free up:
questions.explanation: Disk cleaners can find files that are unnecessary to computer operation, or take up considerable amounts of space. Disk cleaner helps the user to decide what to delete when their hard disk is full.

questions.question: Rearrangement of files and spaces happens through:
questions.explanation: Disk defragmenters detects computer files and disjoints fragments, and move the fragments together to increase efficiency.

questions.question: The fiber optic system is of:
questions.explanation: Fiber optic cabling consists of a center glass core surrounded by several layers of protective materials. It transmits light rather than electronic signals eliminating the problem of electrical interference.

questions.question: Which type of keys are not present on a keyboard?
questions.explanation: Numeric, Alphabetic and function keys are present on the keyboard. Numeric keys are used to type numbers, alphabetic keys for typing alphabets and function keys are used to perform some special functions.

questions.question: CD-ROM stands for:
questions.explanation: CD-ROM (Compact Disk Read Only Memory) has large amount of storage capacity for use with all types of systems. It is put inside the CPU and is visible from the outside.

questions.question: CD-ROM is a:
questions.explanation: A CD-ROM is an optical disc which contains audio or software data whose memory is read-only.

questions.question: Faster data communication is possible when using:
questions.explanation: Fiber optic cable has the ability to transmit signals over much longer distances than coaxial and twisted pair. It also has the capability to carry information at vastly greater speeds.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a primary storage device?
questions.explanation: All options such as magnetic tape, magnetic disk and optical disk are not primary storage device. They are all secondary storage device.

questions.question: Which of the following memory medium is not used as a memory system?
questions.explanation: Magnetic tape is not used as a memory system. Large organisations who need to back up their systems daily tend to use magnetic tapes to store their data. Magnetic tapes can store up to one terabyte of uncompressed data - as much as can be stored on a hard disk. Magnetic tape uses 'serial access' to find a piece of data.

questions.question: What does the disk drive of a computer do?

options.option: Load a program from the disk into the memory

questions.explanation: The disk drive of a computer rotate and read the contents of the disk.

questions.question: The inexpensive form of wiring of data communication is the:
questions.explanation: Twisted pair cable is a most commonly used cable in communication. In this type of cable wires are twisted together in pairs.

questions.question: WAN operate at the speed of less than 1 MBps while LANs operate between:
questions.explanation: The data fetching speed in LAN is 1 to 10 mbps.

questions.question: The device which is used to convert digital data into analog form and vice versa is called a:
questions.explanation: A modem (constructed from modulate and demodulate) is a device that modulates an analog earner signal to encode digital information and also demodulates such a carrier signal to decode the transmitted information. The goal is to produce a signal that can be transmitted easily and decoded to reproduce the original digital data.

questions.question: In LAN, communication between different machines is:
questions.explanation: The communication speed of LAN is fast as compared to other types of networks.

questions.question: In digital computer, data is represented in:
questions.explanation: Digital computer understands only machine language which is a language of the binary digits, i.e. 0s and 1s. Thus, data is represented in binary form that means 0s and 1s.

questions.question: WAN stands for:
questions.explanation: A wide area network is a telecommunications network that extends over a large geographic area for the primary purpose of computer networking.

questions.question: The processing speed of a computer is measured in:
questions.explanation: The speed at which a computer processes information is measured in Megahertz. It is also abbreviated as MHz.

questions.question:
Which of the following does not represent an I/O device?

  1. Speaker
  2. Plotter
  3. ALU
  4. Joystick
questions.explanation: Here, ALU is a processing device. ALU is a part of CPU which calculates all the arithmetic and logical operations.

questions.question: Lowest unit of speed is:
questions.explanation: Picosecond is the lowest unit of speed of a computer. A picosecond is to one second as one second is to approximately 31,689 years.

questions.question: CRT is a type of:
questions.explanation: CRT is a type of monitor. CRT means Cathode Ray Tub. The CRT has a display screen of 25 lines of 80 characters each; it is used in most desktop monitors. Some CRT screens are monochrome (one color) while others produce many colors.

questions.question: Microprocessor was invented in:
questions.explanation: Microprocessors were invented by - Ted Hoff, along with a handful of visionary colleagues working at a young Silicon Valley startup called Intel. It was invented in the year 1971.

questions.question: Microprocessor contains:
questions.explanation: The microprocessor contains the ALU and CU required to perform the functions of a computer's central processing unit. The integrated circuit is capable of interpreting and executing program instructions and performing arithmetic operations.

questions.question: A slow-speed printer like a chain printer is:
questions.explanation: A type of impact printer which contains a metal band with character shapes imprinted on it. During output, the band rotates horizontally past the paper, and a hammer strikes the band opposite the appropriate character, pressing it through an inked ribbon and onto the paper.

questions.question: The first microprocessor was:
questions.explanation: The Intel 4004 is a 4-bit central processing unit released by Intel Corporation in 1971. It was the first commercially produced microprocessor, and the first in a long line of Intel CPUs.

questions.question: A laser printer is:
questions.explanation: A laser printer is a type of nonimpact printer. Non-impact printers were recently developed to meet some of the needs that were not being satisfied by impact technology.

questions.question: Add, Subtract, Multiple and Logic operations are performed by:
questions.explanation: This unit performs two kinds of operations, the arithmetic processing and logical processing. In arithmetic processing it performs all mathematical operations such as addition, subtraction, multiplication and division. In logical processing, it performs the relational and logical operations such as comparing larger or smaller values, true or false statements, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an input device?
questions.explanation: All the devices like Scanner, web camera and keyboard are input devices. An input device is a peripheral device through which data are entered and transformed into machine-readable form.

questions.question: A CPU consists of:
questions.explanation: The central processing unit (CPU) consists of Control Unit, Arithmetic-Logic Unit and primary storage (RAM and ROM).

questions.question: GUI is used as an interface between—
questions.explanation: Graphical User Interface (GUI) acts as interface or mediator between the user and the computer system.

questions.question: The memory can be divided into:
questions.explanation:
The computer's memory is divided into primary memory and secondary memory. In primary storage, there are two types of memory (RAM and ROM). In secondary memory, there are many storage devices like hard disk, CD,
DVD, pen drive, etc.

questions.question: The keyboards we normally use is called:
questions.explanation: One of the most common and familiar input device is the standard keyboard. The traditional QWERTY typewriter comprises the basic portion of today's computer keyboard.

questions.question: The data processing consists:
questions.explanation: Data processing is, generally, "the collection and manipulation of items of data to produce meaningful information." In this sense it can be considered a subset of information processing, "the change of information in any manner detectable by an observer."

questions.question: Which of the following is a version of Android OS?
questions.explanation: Belle, facebook and Vista is not the version of Android.

questions.question: The control unit controls all:
questions.explanation: The Control Unit is a part of the CPU. It controls the overall operations of the computer system. It controls and coordinates all the hardware and software operations as done by the CPU of the computer system.

questions.question: Resolution of a picture is measured in:
questions.explanation: Pixel is the smallest unit of resolution. Resolution is measured in terms of pixels.

questions.question: Daisy wheel printer is a type of:
questions.explanation: Daisy wheel printer is a type of impact printer. An impact printer is a type of printer that works by direct contact of an ink ribbon with paper.

questions.question: A disadvantage of the laser printer is:

options.option: The input is of a lower quality
It is quiet faster than an impact printer
The output is of a lower quality

questions.explanation: The output of the laser printer may be low in comparison with other printers as they print only in monochrome.

questions.question: VDU is used as an:
questions.explanation: A computer display (also known as a computer monitor, computer screen, or computer video display) is a device that can display signals generated by a computer as images on a screen. It is an output device. The full form of VDU is Visual Display Unit.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not an operating system-
questions.explanation: Windows XP, UNIX and DOS are the names of the operating system. COBOL is an application programming language, not an OS.

questions.question: Storage capacity of a DVD is ________.
questions.explanation: Digital Versatile Disk (DVD) is very similar to a CD, but it has a much larger data capacity. A standard DVD holds about seven times more data than a CD does. This huge capacity means that a DVD has enough room to store a full-length movie as well as a lot of other information. The storage capacity of a DVD is 4.7 gigabytes.

questions.question: CAD stands for:
questions.explanation:
Computer-aided design is the use of computers to aid in the creation, modification, analysis, or optimization of a design. This software is used to increase the productivity of the designer, improve the quality of design, improve
communications through documentation, and to create a database for manufacturing.

questions.question: Hard disk is made up of:
questions.explanation: Hard disk is the secondary storage device. It is fixed in the system cabinet. It has a large storage device. It made up of a material called Aluminum.

questions.question: Which one of the following is discontinued operating system-
questions.explanation: Symbian is an operating system that is discontinued. Symbian is a discontinued mobile operating system and computing platform designed for smartphones. Symbian was originally developed as a proprietary software OS for PDAs in 1998 by the Symbian Ltd. consortium.

questions.question: DTP stands for:
questions.explanation: Desktop publishing is the creation of documents using page layout software on a personal computer. It was first used almost exclusively for print publications, but now it also assists in the creation of various forms of online content.

questions.question: What is the full form of BOSS?
questions.explanation: Bharat Operating System Solutions (BOSS) is a free and open source computer operating system developed by the National Resource Centre for Free/Open Source Software (NRCFOSS) of India.

questions.question: Editor is used to:
questions.explanation: Editor is a software used to draw and manipulate graphics, images, text and also used to write the text in an interactive mode.

questions.question: The intersection areas of rows and columns in spreadsheet are called:
questions.explanation: A cell is the smallest unit in a spreadsheet. It is formed by the intersection of rows and columns.

questions.question: Which of the following is a presentation software?
questions.explanation: Presentation graphics software allows users to create graphically rich presentations. One of the most widely used presentation graphics program is Microsoft's PowerPoint.

questions.question:
Ankit Mane is an engineering student and has to design a model for a generator. He should:

  1. Straight-away make the model and test it.
  2. Draw the model on the paper and then make the real model.
  3. Make a clay model and then the actual model.
  4. Use CAD software to draw and test the model and make a real model.
questions.explanation:
Graphics software allows the user to create, store, and display or print charts, graphs, maps, and drawings. Graphics software enables users to absorb more information more quickly and to spot relationships and trends in data more easily. There are three basic categories of graphics software packages: presentation graphics, analysis
graphics, and computer-aided design software.

questions.question: A company's DBMS may be integrated with:
questions.explanation: DBMS is used to store and retrieve the records and it can be used as the back-end support for Payroll system, HR management and Attendance system.

questions.question: ________ operating system is designed and produced by Microsoft Corporation.
questions.explanation: Windows makes a computer system user-friendly by providing a graphical display and organizing information so that it can be easily accessed. The operating system utilizes icons and tools that simplify the complex operations performed by computers.

questions.question: iOS is a mobile operating system developed by ________.
questions.explanation: iOS (previously iPhone OS) is a mobile operating system developed by Apple Inc. and distributed exclusively for Apple hardware. It is the operating system that powers many of the company's iDevices.

questions.question: Linux was developed by ________.
questions.explanation: Linus Torvalds, a young man studying computer science at the University of Helsinki, thought it would be a good idea to have some sort of freely available academic version of Unix, and promptly started to code.

subtopics.name: Safely browsing the web and using social networks

questions.question: Which of the following is considered as cybercrime?
Which of the following terms refers to a person who sends spam mails?
Which of the following creates a barrier between the computer and any unauthorised program trying to come in through the Internet?
Which of the following is a concern related to the information security?
Which of the following viruses can infect an Excel document?
What is the full form of VIRUS?
Which of the following viruses resides in the RAM of a computer system?
Which of the following antiviruses is made by a Chinese company?
Which of the following viruses infects the boot records on the hard disk?
Which of the following is an anitvirus software?
Why should an antivirus be updated regularly?

options.option: In any case, we have to pay for subscription.

questions.question: Which of the following is an expert computer programmer and is not involved in unethical and illegal practices?
Which of the following is a computer program that enters into the computer without the user’s knowledge?
For how many years is a trademark valid?

If someone says they are 16 you should:

  1. believe them, they have no reason to lie
  2. not trust anyone you don’t personally know
  3. give them your age
  4. meet up with them because they may be someone cool to hang out with
questions.explanation: not trust anyone you don’t personally know

questions.question: Who is the most common victim of online predators?

Which among the following is not a malicious website?

  1. Address, Phone Number and/or E-mail address is present.
  2. The website address is not mis-spelled.
  3. The https:// at the beginning of the website.
  4. The link shows a different address than what is mentioned.
questions.explanation: The https:// at the beginning of the website.

questions.question: Cyber safety is the responsible use of
questions.explanation: Cyber safety is the safe and responsible use of Information and Communication Technology (ICT).

questions.question: ________ stands for Malicious Software is software designed to infiltrate a computer system without the owner's informed consent.
questions.explanation: Malware designed to interrupt ongoing computer operations, collect private information and gain unauthorised access to your system resources.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a malware?
________ refers to the condition in which your computer stops working.
questions.explanation: Hang refers to the condition in which your computer or software stops working.

questions.question:
Which of the following is not a symptom of a virus attack?

  1. The computer begins to run slowly
  2. Unusual messages and graphics appear on the screen for inexplicable reasons
  3. Some programs or data files on the computer either become corrupt or are difficult to locate
  4. Your data is copied to another location
questions.explanation: Your data is copied to another location is not a symptom of a virus attack.

questions.question: A good way to safeguard access to your computer to protect it.
questions.explanation: Password is a good way to safeguard access to your computer to protect it.

questions.question: Which of the following is the first computer virus?
questions.explanation: Elk Cloner is the first computer virus created by Richard Skrenta.

questions.question: Out of the following, which one will be altered or modified by a computer virus?
Which one of the following is not a way to get a computer virus?
questions.explanation: By typing on keyboard is not a way to get a computer virus.

questions.question: It is an electronic messaging system used by various commercial organisations to send bulk messages to a large number of e-mail addresses. What is it?
Which of the following is not an example of worm?
questions.explanation: De bumper is a type of trojan horse.

questions.question: A category of software that spies of your computer activities is
questions.explanation: Spyware is a category of software that spies computer activities.

questions.question: The electronic equivalent of junk mail is known as
questions.explanation: Spam is an electronic equivalent of junk mail.

questions.question: What is Cyber Security?

options.option: Cyber Security protects a system from cyber attacks

questions.explanation: Cyber Security provides security to a system against cyber-attacks by using various technologies and processes.

questions.question: Who is the father of computer security?
questions.explanation: August Kerckhoffs, a linguist and German professor at HEC, wrote an essay in the Journal of Military Science in February 1883. Kerckhoff had unwittingly established the foundations for contemporary encryption, earning him the title of "Father of Computer Security."

questions.question: Which of the following is not a cybercrime?
questions.explanation: Denial of Service, Man in the Middle, and Malware exploit the system causing a threat to security, hence they are considered as cybercrime. AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) provides security by encrypting the data.

questions.question: In which of the following, a person is constantly followed/chased by another person or group of several peoples?
questions.explanation: In general, Stalking refers to continuous surveillance on the target (or person) done by a group of people or by the individual person.

questions.question: Which of the following usually observe each activity on the internet of the victim, gather all information in the background and send it to someone else?
questions.explanation: It is generally defined as the software designed to enter the target's device or computer system., gather all information, observe all user activities, and send this information to a third party. Another important thing about the spyware is that it works in the background sends all information without your permission.

questions.question: ________ is a type of software designed to help the user’s computer detect viruses and avoid them.
questions.explanation: An antivirus is a kind of software that is specially designed to help the user's computer to detect the virus as well as to avoid the harmful effect of them.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a type of antivirus program?
questions.explanation: Antivirus is a kind of software program that helps to detect and remove viruses form the user's computer and provides a safe environment for users to work on.

questions.question: Which one of the following is also referred to as malicious software?
questions.explanation: Malware is a kind of short program used by the hacker to gain access to sensitive data/ information. It is used to denote many kinds of viruses, worms, Trojans and several other harmful programs. Sometimes malware is also known as malicious software.

questions.question: A term that is used in spreadsheet which forces a line break in a cell when the cell entry is too large to fit in the cell.
The default alignment of text in a cell is:
________ splits text in columns at a specific point.
questions.explanation: Column break splits text in columns at a specific point.

questions.question: It is a section of the document that appears at the bottom margin of the page.
questions.explanation: Footer is the section of the document that appears at the bottom margin of the page.

questions.question: Spelling & Grammar option from the Proofing group comes under
questions.explanation: Spelling & Grammar option from the Proofing group comes under Review tab.

questions.question: The Insert tab Symbol option from Symbols group is used to
questions.explanation: Insert tab Symbol option from Symbols group is used to insert symbols in a document.

questions.question: In the Print dialog box, you can select the pages for printing.
questions.explanation: You can select the even, odd and all pages in range for printing.

questions.question: Which shortcut key is used to make the selected text italic?
questions.explanation: Ctrl + I is the shortcut key to make the selected text italic.

questions.question: We can underline the text by
questions.explanation: Ctrl+U is the shortcut key for underlining the text.

questions.question: Which extension is given to a document by default in MS-Word?
questions.explanation: .docx is the default extension of MS-Word 2007 document.

questions.question: The status of your document like current page number and number of pages are given by ________.
questions.explanation: Status bar gives the status of your document like current page number and number of pages.

questions.question: A new text document can be created by
questions.explanation:
Steps to create a new document are
File {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} New {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Blank Document {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Create

questions.question: The documents can be saved by using
questions.explanation: Documents can be saved by clicking File {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Save As, File {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Save or using Ctrl+S keys.

questions.question: The general arrangement of the text in the document is ________.
questions.explanation: Formatting is the general arrangement of the text in the document.

questions.question: Coloring of an illustration or diagram with parallel lines is known as
questions.explanation: Shading is the darkening or coloring of an illustration or diagram with parallel lines or a block of color.

questions.question: It is a grid of cells arranged in rows and columns.
questions.explanation: Table is a grid of cells arranged in rows and columns.

questions.question: The Table Properties dialog box includes tabs for
questions.explanation: The Table Properties dialog box contains four tabs namely Table, Row, Column and Cell.

questions.question: Which tab of MS Word contains the Shapes option?
questions.explanation: Insert tab of MS Word contains the Shapes option.

questions.question: Which of the following features is used to revert the original picture?
questions.explanation: Reset picture feature of Picture Tools Format is used to revert the original picture.

questions.question: What is the default left margin in Word 2003 document?
questions.explanation:
In Word 2003 and earlier, the default top and bottom margins were 1 inch (25.4 mm), but 1.25 inches (31.7 mm) were given at the left and the right.
The default margins for Microsoft Word from version 2007 onward have been 1 inch (25.4 mm) all around.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a font style?
questions.explanation: A superscript is a character, symbol or number set slightly above the normal line of text. It is always smaller than the usual font and is typically found in mathematical or scientific formulas, it is effects to the font.

questions.question: Excel can open a new workbook by:
questions.explanation: To open a new workbook, File {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} New command or Ctrl + N keys are used.

questions.question: Color and pattern used to fill a closed shape is called
In Excel, by default numbers are aligned to:
questions.explanation: While entering numbers in the cells of the worksheet, the numbers automatically gets right aligned.

questions.question: To autofit the width of column:
questions.explanation: To make the columns in a table automatically fit the contents, click on our table. On the Layout tab, in the Cell Size group, click AutoFit, and then click AutoFit Contents, or double click the right border of column.

questions.question: Excel contains the maximum number of spread-sheet in a workbook:
questions.explanation: A workbook contains many worksheets. By default, 255 worksheets are present in a workbook.

questions.question: The key F12 opens a:
questions.explanation: The key F12 opens a Save As dialog box.

questions.question: The whole worksheet can be selected by:
questions.explanation: By pressing Ctrl + A keys, all the data already in a worksheet gets selected.

questions.question: Ctrl + S is used to
questions.explanation: Ctrl + S is used to Save Document with same name.

questions.question: Ctrl + Home is used to:

options.option: Moves the cursor to the beginning of Document
Moves the cursor to the beginning of Line
Moves the cursor to the beginning of Paragraph
Moves the cursor to the beginning of Word

questions.explanation: "Ctrl + Home" is used to moves the cursor to the beginning of Document.

questions.question: You can save Excel workbook file by pressing:
questions.explanation: The user should save their work for future use. The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + S keys are used to save the changes done in a worksheet.

questions.question: Which of the following is not valid version of MS Office?
questions.explanation: Office Vista is not valid version of MS Office.

questions.question: By default, on which page the header or the footer is printed?
questions.explanation: A header is the top margin of each page, and a footer is the bottom margin of each page. Headers and footers are useful for including material that we want to appear on every page of a document such as your name, the title of the document, or page numbers.

questions.question: Which of the following are word processing software?
questions.explanation: WordPerfect, Wordpad and MS Word are word processing software. These are used to manipulate a text document, such as a resume or a report. We typically enter text by typing, and the software provides tools for copying, deleting and various types of formatting.

questions.question: In Excel, worksheet can be named upto:
questions.explanation: An Excel worksheet can be named with a maximum of 31 characters.

questions.question: The space left between the margin and the start of a paragraph is called:
If you are working with Excel, you can underline text by pressing key:
questions.explanation: Entered text can be underlined by firstly selecting the cells containing the text and then pressing Ctrl + U keys.

questions.question: A number of letter that appears little above the normal text is called:
When you create a formula, all the formula starts with:
questions.explanation: All the formula/function in Excel starts with an equal to(=) sign.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a view in Excel?
questions.explanation: Reading view is not a view in Excel. The different views in Excel are: Normal view, Page Layout view, Page Break Preview and Full-screen view.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the error ####?
questions.explanation: The error "####" means that the column is not wide enough to display the content. Increase column width, shrink contents to fit the column, or apply a different number format.

questions.question: ________ is an animated presentation that we display on a computer monitor or projection system.
questions.explanation: To view a presentation on full screen is called slide show.

questions.question: Which type of chart characterizes only one series as a series of slices?
questions.explanation: Pie chart characterizes only one data series as a series of slices, each contributing to a whole.

questions.question: Select the font size from:
questions.explanation: The font size of the selected text can be changed by using the font size option from the Font group of Home tab.

questions.question: You can hide the slide from:
questions.explanation: A slide can be made hidden using the Tools menu of the presentation software.

questions.question: How can you insert a Sound file in a Presentation?

options.option: By right clicking the document and choosing

questions.explanation: Sound can be inserted onto a slide by using Insert menu and then clicking on Audio option.

questions.question: PowerPoint software is used for:
questions.explanation: PowerPoint is used for creating attractive presentation. A presentation may contain many slides in it.

questions.question: If you have chosen a slide background but you want to change the colour scheme, you will find the relevant option under:
questions.explanation: To change the color scheme of the slide background, the Format menu is used.

questions.question: A picture on a slide can be inserted from:
questions.explanation: A picture on a slide can be inserted from many sources like file, clipart or web.

questions.question: A slide can have:
questions.explanation: A slide can contain all the elements like text, picture, audio, video on it which can be viewed on full screen.

questions.question: While running a slide show, transition of slides can be done:
questions.explanation: The slide transition can take place manually (by pressing the keyboard key or pressing mouse button) or using Rehearse timing feature (recording the time upto which a presentation will be viewed).

questions.question: Clipart is a product of:
questions.explanation: Clipart is a feature of Microsoft. It is included in almost all the versions of the MS Office package.

questions.question: In PowerPoint you can colour:
questions.explanation: In PowerPoint, different fonts, lines and shadows can be coloured according to the choice of the user.

questions.question: The text object pointed in shape with:
questions.explanation: The text object can be pointed by a mouse pointer that be of 2 headed, 3 headed or 4 headed.

questions.question: The page setting appears in:
questions.explanation: The option to set the page or slide in a presentation appears in File menu.

questions.question: For moving slide to previous slide:
questions.explanation: A presentation may contain many slides in it. To move to the previous slide from the current slide, PageUp key is pressed on the keyboard.

questions.question: You can display first slide by:
questions.explanation: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl + F4 keys are used to move to the first slide of the presentation.

questions.question: A word processor would most likely be used to do:

options.option: Do a computer search in media center

questions.explanation: A word processor, or word processing program, does exactly what the name implies. It is used to manipulate a text document, such as a resume or a report. We typically enter text by typing, and the software provides tools for copying, deleting and various types of formatting. So in given options it would most likely be used to do type a biography.

questions.question: Which can be used for quick access to commonly used commands and tools?
questions.explanation: In Word 2003, Tool bar is used for quick access to commonly used commands and tools.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an Autoshape?
questions.explanation: ClipArt is not an Autoshape, because it is not found under Shapes.

questions.question: Which of the following options is used to control the Automatic formatting that the user wants?
questions.explanation: For turning Automatic formatting On or Off, AutoFormat As You Type tab under Proofing is used.

questions.question: AutoFormat settings are applied in a document

options.option: both as we type text and after we finish typing

questions.explanation: AutoFormat settings are applied in a document as we type text or after we finish typing.

questions.question: ________ feature allows you to view different items such as comments, formatting etc, and also provides the option to view other author's comments.
questions.explanation: Show Markup feature under Review tab allows you to view different items.

questions.question: Under which tab, Comments group is present.
Which of the following charts selects only one range of the data series?
questions.explanation: Pie chart can select only one range of the data series.

questions.question: Which feature is used to monitor all document changes?
questions.explanation: Track changes tool is used when a document is edited or reviewed by many people.

questions.question: The another name for the XY chart is:
The sample of an Excel worksheet on the screen can be seen by
questions.explanation: Print preview enables you to view the sample of worksheet before printing it on a paper.

questions.question: Which of the following shortcut keys brings the cursor to the last cell on the sheet that contains data?
questions.explanation: Ctrl + End shortcut key is used to bring the cursor to the last cell.

questions.question: For selecting a non-continuous range of cells, you need to do what?
questions.explanation: By pressing Ctrl key and clicking on the desired cell a non-continuous cell range can be selected.

questions.question: For selecting the entire worksheet in Excel, you need to press
questions.explanation: You need to press Ctrl + Shift + Spacebar for selecting the entire worksheet in Excel.

questions.question: ________ is the default file name for a PowerPoint presentation.
questions.explanation: The default name given to a presentation file is Presentation 1.

questions.question: File extension for a PowerPoint 2007 presentation is
questions.explanation: .pptx is the file extension given to all PowerPoint 2007 files.

questions.question: The custom animation can apply
questions.explanation: The custom animation can apply font work gallery, gallery and text.

questions.question: Professional looking visual aids are prepared with the help of software called ________.
questions.explanation: Professional looking visual aids are prepared with the help of software called Presentation graphics software.

questions.question: A set of predefined formats of text or color scheme is called
questions.explanation: A set of predefined formats of text or color scheme is called theme.

questions.question: Which of the following views is useful for representing the structure of a presentation?
questions.explanation: Outline view is useful for checking the flow or structure of a presentation.

questions.question: The entire presentation can be seen at a time in
questions.explanation: Slide Sorter view enables you to view the entire presentation at a time.

questions.question: Which among the following views allows you to give a thumbnail view of all the slides in a presentation?
questions.explanation: Slide Sorter view provides a view of slides in thumbnail form.

questions.question: Which among the following views allows you to add some extra information to a slide which is not viewed during the presentation.
questions.explanation: Notes Page view allows you to add some extra information related to presentation but it is not viewed during the presentation.

questions.question: Which file format can be added to a PowerPoint show?
questions.explanation: .jpg and .gif are file extensions for images and .wav is an extension for video file. Hence, all of these formats are supported in PowerPoint.

questions.question: Ramu is preparing a presentation on her company's annual performance. She wants some text present on a slide to fly in from the left, when she clicks the mouse. Which of the following features should she use?
questions.explanation: Custom Animation can be used for the desired functionality.

questions.question: Special effects used to introduce slides in a presentation are
questions.explanation: Special effects used to introduce slides in a presentation are known as transitions.

questions.question: What is the shortcut key to display the Microsoft PowerPoint shortcut menu?
questions.explanation: Shift + F10 is the shortcut key to display the PowerPoint shortcut menu.

questions.question: Which of the following shortcut keys is used to select all content of a file?
questions.explanation: Ctrl + A will select all the items or text available on the screen.
